name: Operations and Observability
summary: Operations runbook, private networking, mesh posture, backup/restore visibility, incident posture, and operator observability contracts.
invariants: Operational tooling and observability do not create clinical, Core, or regulatory authority.
Transport, mesh, and backup posture are not authorization.
Incidents and degradation must be visible and recorded, not silently hidden.
Observability payloads must avoid direct identifiers and secrets.
allowedWork: Maintain runbooks, incident vocabulary, network posture, backup/restore docs, and observability contracts.
Add validation scripts only when scoped and documented.
Clarify operational degradation and residual gaps.
Keep local-first and private-network assumptions explicit.
forbiddenWork: Treat incident tooling as clinical authority.
Claim backup, restore, network, or fabric production hardening without evidence.
knownGaps: Production operations automation and restore-drill evidence remain future work.
Operational fabric hardening remains outside scaffold claims.
notes: The `ops` path may be absent or minimal in some snapshots; verify before edits.
Territory metadata does not operate infrastructure.
